title: Astronomy for Mortals
subtitle: Where infinity becomes understandable
author: Wordsus Team
description: A fascinating journey through the cosmos in 52 weekly lessons. From the solar system to the far reaches of the observable universe, discover how space-time works, the stars and the great mysteries of astronomy explained in a simple way.

chapters.title: The Phantom of Space: The Hunt for the Hidden Planet Nine
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating mystery of Planet Nine, a hypothetical ice giant that might be hiding in the dark, outer reaches of our own Solar System.

chapters.title: Vanishing Beauty: The Inevitable End of Saturn's Rings
chapters.description: Discover why the famous gas giant is slowly losing its majestic ring system due to the relentless force of gravity pulling them down into the planet.

chapters.title: The Eternal Storm: Exploring the Furious Eye of Jupiter
chapters.description: We delve into Jupiter's Great Red Spot, a monstrous anticyclonic storm the exact size of Earth that has been active for centuries without ever stopping.

chapters.title: The Solar System's Roof: Climbing the Martian Volcano
chapters.description: We travel to Olympus Mons on Mars, the most colossal volcano in our entire Solar System, whose immense height easily triples that of Mount Everest.

chapters.title: Hidden Seas: Icy Worlds and Liquid Water in Deep Space
chapters.description: Let's dive into the underground oceans of icy moons like Europa and Enceladus, distant worlds hiding vast liquid seas beneath thick, freezing crusts.

chapters.title: The System's Shell: Exploring the Mysterious Oort Cloud
chapters.description: We travel to the Oort Cloud, the distant and mysterious outer sphere that surrounds our system and serves as the original home of long-period comets.

chapters.title: Cosmic Shadows: The Perfect Mechanics of Eclipses
chapters.description: We clearly explain the fascinating mechanics of eclipses, detailing how the perfect alignment between the Earth, the Moon, and the Sun creates them.

chapters.title: Debris from the Past: Inside the Great Asteroid Belt
chapters.description: We navigate through the Asteroid Belt, a massive ring of rocky debris located between Mars and Jupiter that never managed to group into a new planet.

chapters.title: The Sky on Fire: The Spectacular Cosmic Meteor Showers
chapters.description: We observe meteor showers, dazzling astronomical phenomena produced when our planet swiftly crosses the trails of cosmic dust left by passing comets.

chapters.title: The Small Worlds: Pluto and the Family of Dwarf Planets
chapters.description: We explore Pluto and the family of dwarf planets in the Kuiper Belt, small icy worlds that completely redefined our understanding of the Solar System.

chapters.title: Stellar Nurseries: The Cosmic Cradles of Newborn Stars
chapters.description: We admire nebulae, immense clouds of space dust and gas, like the Pillars of Creation, which function as literal factories where new stars are born.

chapters.title: The Final Outburst: The Fury of the Giant Supernovae
chapters.description: We witness the raw power of supernovae, colossal explosions marking the death of massive stars that can briefly outshine an entire galaxy combined.

chapters.title: Dense Hearts: Unraveling the Enigma of Neutron Stars
chapters.description: We analyze neutron stars, extremely dense stellar remnants where a single teaspoon of their unique matter would weigh millions of tons here on Earth.

chapters.title: Cosmic Lighthouses: The Dizzying Spin of Pulsars
chapters.description: We decipher pulsars, incredible neutron stars that spin at lightning speeds, emitting powerful beams of bright light just like lighthouses in space.

chapters.title: Modest Destinies: The White Dwarfs and the Red Dwarfs
chapters.description: We compare the white dwarf future awaiting our Sun and the vital life cycle of red dwarfs, the most incredibly abundant stars in the entire universe.

chapters.title: Suns in Pairs: The Cosmic Dance of Multiple Star Systems
chapters.description: We contemplate fascinating multiple star systems, distant worlds with double or triple suns that intertwine in an infinite, beautiful orbital dance.

chapters.title: Celestial Monsters: The Terror of the Hypergiant Stars
chapters.description: We measure hypergiant stars like UY Scuti, astronomical bodies so colossal they would easily engulf Jupiter's entire orbit if placed in our system.

chapters.title: The End of Our Days: Exploring the Final Fate of the Sun
chapters.description: We project the ultimate destiny of our Sun and how, after becoming a fearsome red giant, it will end its long days shrinking into a white dwarf star.

chapters.title: Spheres of Antiquity: The Mystery of Star Globular Clusters
chapters.description: We navigate through dense globular clusters, gravity-packed spheres grouping hundreds of thousands of the oldest stars found anywhere in the universe.

chapters.title: Solitary Suns: The Dark Journey of the Wandering Stars
chapters.description: We follow wandering stars, solitary suns that have been violently expelled from their native galaxies and now roam lost through deep intergalactic space.

chapters.title: Absolute Darkness: An X-Ray Vision of a Dark Black Hole
chapters.description: We unravel the terrifying anatomy of a black hole, from its glowing accretion disk to the pitch-black event horizon that traps absolutely all light.

chapters.title: Galactic Hearts: Exploring Supermassive Black Holes
chapters.description: We explore supermassive black holes, immense cosmic colossi like Sagittarius A* that govern hidden deep within the heart of almost every known galaxy.

chapters.title: Shredded by Gravity: The Dark Spaghettification Effect
chapters.description: We analyze spaghettification, the real astrophysical effect where the extreme gravity of a black hole violently stretches objects into long, thin shapes.

chapters.title: The Clash of Titans: Enormous Black Hole Collisions
chapters.description: We recount the violent collisions of massive black holes, catastrophic cosmic events so immense they manage to generate powerful gravitational waves.

chapters.title: Beacons of the Past: The Bright Glow of the Great Quasars
chapters.description: We dazzle our eyes with quasars, the most luminous objects in the universe driven by feeding black holes that relentlessly devour surrounding matter.

chapters.title: Cosmic Gunshots: Deadly Blazars and Their Energy Beams
chapters.description: We study blazars, an extreme type of quasar whose dizzyingly fast jet of matter and incredibly bright light travels pointing directly toward the Earth.

chapters.title: The Monster's Feast: Spectacular Tidal Disruption Events
chapters.description: We observe tidal disruption events, the brilliantly bright flash of light that occurs when a black hole mercilessly shreds and swallows a nearby star.

chapters.title: Fountains of Light: The Fascinating Myth of White Holes
chapters.description: We speculate on the astonishing theory of white holes, the mysterious and exact opposites of black holes that would supposedly expel matter endlessly.

chapters.title: Extreme Magnets: The Immense Destructive Power of Magnetars
chapters.description: We feel the force of magnetars, a peculiar type of neutron star that possesses the most incredibly powerful and destructive magnetic field in the cosmos.

chapters.title: Flashes of the End: The Cosmic Gamma-Ray Burst Eruptions
chapters.description: We investigate deadly gamma-ray bursts, the most highly energetic flashes of astronomical light capable of outshining entire galaxies for a few seconds.

chapters.title: Our Spiral Home: A Grand Journey Through the Milky Way
chapters.description: We visually map a route through the Milky Way, analyzing the beautiful spiral structure of our galaxy and the quiet location of our very own stellar arm.

chapters.title: Imminent Clash: Andromeda and Our Ultimate Galactic End
chapters.description: We anticipate the epic collision between Andromeda and the Milky Way, a galactic impact that will inevitably change our observable night sky forever.

chapters.title: The Space Zoo: Discovering the Different Galaxy Types
chapters.description: We classify the enormous diversity of galaxies in the cosmos, from the most majestic and beautiful spirals to immense ellipticals and irregular shapes.

chapters.title: Close Neighborhood: Exploring the Borders of the Local Group
chapters.description: We get to know the Local Group, our astronomical neighborhood bound by gravity that we directly share with Andromeda and the Triangulum Galaxy today.

chapters.title: Colossal Structures: Exploring the Galaxy Superclusters
chapters.description: We measure the scale of galactic superclusters like Laniakea, immense cosmic structures that group hundreds of thousands of galaxies moving together.

chapters.title: The Web of Everything: Navigating the Giant Cosmic Web
chapters.description: We contemplate the incredible cosmic web, the colossal overarching structure of the universe where galaxy-filled filaments connect like a giant spiderweb.

chapters.title: Absolute Nothingness: The Deep Mystery of Cosmic Voids
chapters.description: We dive into the freezing cosmic voids, immense dark regions of deep space like the Boötes Void that present an unusually rare shortage of galaxies.

chapters.title: The Growing Cosmos: The Great Expansion of the Universe
chapters.description: We explain the ceaseless expansion of the universe, reviewing Edwin Hubble's fundamental observation that proved that galaxies are moving further apart.

chapters.title: Southern Lights: The Magellanic Clouds and Their Mystery
chapters.description: We admire the Magellanic Clouds, two fascinating dwarf galaxies that orbit the Milky Way and are perfectly visible to the naked eye from planet Earth.

chapters.title: Sun Factories: The Galaxies with Intense Starbursts
chapters.description: We witness the activity of Starburst galaxies, astronomical formations that are currently experiencing an exaggeratedly high rate of new star creation.

chapters.title: Promising Worlds: Planets Hidden in the Habitable Zone
chapters.description: We search for exoplanets in the habitable zone, worlds orbiting at the perfect distance from their host star to allow for liquid water on their surface.

chapters.title: Gaseous Hells: Exploring the Reign of the Hot Jupiters
chapters.description: We explore Hot Jupiters, gigantic gas planets that orbit so incredibly close to their mother star that one full year there passes in just a few days.

chapters.title: Void Wanderers: The Dark Drama of Lonely Rogue Planets
chapters.description: We follow the dark path of rogue planets, solitary worlds that float freely through the vast interstellar emptiness without ever orbiting a parent star.

chapters.title: The Eye of Humanity: The Amazing James Webb Telescope
chapters.description: We celebrate the astronomical wonders of the James Webb Space Telescope, reviewing its infrared discoveries that reveal the most primitive old galaxies.

chapters.title: A Glimpse into the Abyss: The Hubble Deep Field Images
chapters.description: We remember the invaluable legacy of Hubble's Deep Fields, where aiming at apparent emptiness revealed thousands of galaxies hidden in absolute darkness.

chapters.title: Distant Satellites: The Difficult Search for Exomoons
chapters.description: We share the highly complicated astronomical challenge of discovering exomoons, those tiny and elusive satellites orbiting distant exoplanets far away.

chapters.title: The Great Celestial Map: The Extraordinary Gaia Mission
chapters.description: We detail the goals of the amazing Gaia mission, a colossal scientific effort aimed at creating a three-dimensional map of a billion local stars today.

chapters.title: The Galactic Magnet: The Mystery of the Great Attractor
chapters.description: We try to comprehend the enigmatic Great Attractor, the immense and hidden gravitational anomaly that inexorably pulls the Milky Way at high velocities.

chapters.title: Polluted Orbit: The Growing Problem of Space Debris
chapters.description: We address the ever-growing astronomical problem of space debris, a dangerous cloud formed by the scattered remains of inactive satellites orbiting Earth.

chapters.title: Revealing Shadows: Understanding the Transit Method
chapters.description: We explain step by step the ingenious transit method, a key technique widely used in astronomy to find new worlds by observing their minuscule eclipses.

chapters.title: Magnifiers of the Universe: Using Gravitational Lenses
chapters.description: We decipher the great phenomenon of gravitational lenses, colossal masses that manage to bend light, allowing us to observe distant galaxies behind them.

chapters.title: Fleeting Visitors: The Interstellar Asteroid Oumuamua
chapters.description: We analyze the trajectory of interstellar asteroids, mysterious traveling rocks originating from completely different solar systems crossing our cosmic area.
